code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test20-JAN-2016Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
Renal function is mildly impaired (elevated creatinine), with significant proteinuria and low urine specific gravity, suggesting possible chronic kidney disease. Urinalysis shows glucosuria, ketonuria, and bilirubinuria, but no evidence of infection. HbA1c is low, indicating no chronic hyperglycemia. Urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal) may suggest underlying renal or hepatic pathology. Recommend further nephrological evaluation.
